# Configure service discovery

You have two options for service discovery:

- Consul service discovery, which requires access to a Consul cluster. Consul is
  the default option for service discovery.
- Nomad service discovery, which requires no additional infrastructure.

For a comparison of service discovery options and use cases, refer to the
[Service discovery in Nomad documentation](/nomad/docs/networking/service-discovery).

## Common configuration

This section highlights configuration that is common to both Consul and Nomad
service discovery.

### Declare the service discovery provider

In both options, use the [`service`
block](/nomad/docs/job-specification/service), with the [`provider` parameter](/nomad/docs/job-specification/service#provider)
declaring the service discovery option to use. 

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Consul" group="consul">

This example uses Consul service discovery. 

```hcl
job "database" {
  # ...
  group "..." {
    service {
      name     = "database"
      port     = "db"
      provider = "consul"
      # ...
    }
    # ...
  }
}
```

You may omit the `provider` parameter because `consul` is the default service
discovery provider.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Nomad" group="nomad">

This example uses Nomad service discovery.

```hcl
job "database" {
  # ...
  group "..." {
    service {
      name     = "database"
      port     = "db"
      provider = "nomad"
      # ...
    }
    # ...
  }
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>


### Access services

To access other services, use [`template`
blocks](/nomad/docs/job-specification/template#examples) blocks with a Consul
Template service function to query the service discovery catalog.

 The `template` can then be used as a
configuration file or have its content loaded as environment variables to
configure connection information in applications.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="Consul" group="consul">

Use the Consul Template [`service`
function](/consul/docs/reference/consul-template/go#service) to locate services
in the Consul service discovery catalog.

In this example, the "api" job uses the `service` function to locate a service
called "database".

```hcl
job "api" {
  # ...
  group "..." {
    task "api" {
      template {
        data = <<EOF
{{ range service "database" }}
DB_CONNECTION="host={{ .Address }} port={{ .Port }} user=user password=password dbname=db_name"
{{ end }}
EOF
        destination = "local/env.txt"
        env         = true
      }
    }
    # ...
  }
}
```

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Nomad" group="nomad">

https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/docs/reference/consul-template/go#nomadservice

Use the Consul Template [`nomadService`
function](/consul/docs/reference/consul-template/go#service) to locate services
in the Nomad service discovery catalog.

In this example, the "api" job uses the `service` function to locate a service
called "database".

https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/job-specification/template#nomad-services



```hcl
job "api" {
  # ...
  group "api" {
    task "api" {
      template {
        data = <<EOF
{{ range nomadService "database" }}
DB_CONNECTION="host={{ .Address }} port={{ .Port }} user=user password=password dbname=db_name"
{{ end }}
EOF
        destination = "local/env.txt"
        env         = true
      }
    }
    # ...
  }
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>
